page: Vultr vc2-8c-32gb is a Cloud Compute virtual server featuring 8 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Broadwell architecture processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a local 640 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this server type.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores reach average levels in the middle 50% tier. Memory read bandwidth is average for both small and large blocks, though large block memory write bandwidth is poor. Database operations and static web serving throughput are categorized as weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

Given the shared CPU allocation and the older Intel Broadwell processor architecture, this server offers a cost-effective option for resource-dense workloads that require significant memory and storage capacity without demanding sustained, high-performance single-core compute. The 640 GB NVMe SSD provides ample local storage space. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded background processing, development environments, and moderate Redis caching deployments.
description: Vultr vc2-8c-32gb is a Cloud Compute virtual server configured with 8 shared vCPUs on an Intel Broadwell x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 640 GB NVMe SSD. The server features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between poor single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities. While memory read bandwidth is average, memory write bandwidth and database operations are weak. Lacking a GPU, this instance is designed as a budget-friendly option for workloads requiring substantial memory and local storage without needing high single-threaded compute power. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded background tasks, and development environments.
og_description: Vultr vc2-8c-32gb is a Cloud Compute server with 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 640 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced memory and storage for general-purpose and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server offering balanced memory and substantial local NVMe storage for multi-threaded workloads and general-purpose applications.

32.0 GB of system memory with 4.0 GB per core

Suited for general-purpose, multi-threaded, and development workloads
